title: MoneyWorks Import Foundation - Create/Update Records via SDK
description: Implement write operations to create and update records in MoneyWorks through the SDK. This includes adding import methods to SmartMoneyWorksClient, creating write-capable repository base class, implementing validation before write, and exposing import endpoints via ElysiaJS REST API with proper error handling.
why: Enable bidirectional data sync by allowing applications to write back to MoneyWorks, completing the data integration story and enabling real business automation workflows.

tasks.title: Enhance ImportOptions and ImportResult types in canonical layer
tasks.description: Update packages/data/src/client/types.ts with enhanced ImportOptions (mode: insert|update|replace, workItOut, calculated, returnImported) and expand ImportResult with errorDetails array containing record index, field, and message. Add MoneyWorks-specific import mode semantics documentation.

tasks.title: Implement smartImport() in SmartMoneyWorksClient
tasks.description: Add smartImport(table, records, options) method to packages/data/src/client/moneyworks-smart-client.ts. Method must: (1) discover table structure via ensureFieldStructure(), (2) convert objects to TSV with correct field ordering using discovered schema, (3) call base import() method, (4) return typed ImportResult. Include proper error handling for schema discovery failures.

tasks.title: Create validation utilities for pre-write validation
tasks.description: Create packages/data/src/validators/import-validator.ts with: (1) validateRecordForImport(record, schema) function, (2) field type validators (string length, number range, date format), (3) required field checking, (4) ValidationError class with field-level error details. Use canonical field definitions where available.

tasks.title: Implement create/update/upsert in BaseMoneyWorksRepository
tasks.description: Update packages/data/src/repositories/base.repository.ts to implement create(), update(), and add upsert() methods. Methods must: (1) use smartImport with appropriate mode (insert/update/createOrUpdate), (2) call prepare() for data transformation, (3) optionally validate via import-validator, (4) return created/updated record by re-fetching. Replace TODO placeholders with real implementation.

tasks.title: Add import() method to BaseTableController
tasks.description: Update packages/api/src/controllers/base-table.ts to add import(params) method that: (1) accepts ImportParams with records array and ImportOptions, (2) validates authentication, (3) calls repository create/upsert, (4) returns ImportResult. Add validateImport() for input validation.

tasks.title: Create POST /api/v1/{table}/import route
tasks.description: Add import route to packages/api/src/routes/ (either in existing table routes or new import.ts). Route must: (1) use POST method, (2) accept JSON body with records array and options, (3) authenticate request, (4) call controller.import(), (5) return ImportResult with proper HTTP status codes (200 success, 400 validation error, 500 MW error).

tasks.title: Update package exports for new modules
tasks.description: Update packages/data/src/index.ts to export: (1) new validator module, (2) enhanced types. Follow triple-export-registration pattern. Ensure SmartMoneyWorksClient changes are reflected in type definitions.

tasks.title: Run typecheck and fix any compilation errors
tasks.description: Execute 'bun run typecheck' from repository root. Fix any TypeScript errors across all packages. Ensure all imports resolve correctly and type definitions are complete. Document any quirks discovered.

acceptanceCriteria.description: SmartMoneyWorksClient has smartImport() method that converts objects to TSV with correct field ordering based on discovered schema
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: packages/data/src/client/moneyworks-smart-client.ts - smartImport() method at line 253-293, recordsToOrderedTSV() at line 303-359, valueToTSV() at line 365-384

acceptanceCriteria.description: ImportOptions supports mode parameter (insert, update, replace) with proper MoneyWorks semantics
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: packages/data/src/client/types.ts - ImportMode type at line 18 with 'insert' | 'update' | 'replace', ImportOptions interface at line 31-84 with documented mode semantics

acceptanceCriteria.description: BaseWriteRepository class provides create(), update(), and upsert() methods with proper error handling
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: packages/data/src/repositories/base.repository.ts - create() at line 183-221, update() at line 236-276, upsert() at line 295-336, bulkCreate() at line 353-370, bulkUpsert() at line 387-404

acceptanceCriteria.description: Pre-write validation using canonical field validators prevents invalid data from being sent to MoneyWorks
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: packages/data/src/validators/import-validator.ts - validateRecordForImport() at line 269-322, validateRecordsForImport() at line 340-368, validators for string length, number range, date format, required fields

acceptanceCriteria.description: API exposes POST /api/v1/{table}/import endpoint with proper authentication and error responses
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: packages/api/src/routes/tables.ts - POST /:table/import route at line 232-334, TableImportBodySchema and ImportResultSchema in packages/api/src/schemas/table.ts

acceptanceCriteria.description: ImportResult type provides detailed feedback: processed, created, updated, errors count with errorDetails array
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: packages/data/src/client/types.ts - ImportResult interface at line 110-143 with success, processed, created, updated, skipped, errors, errorDetails[], ImportErrorDetail at line 92-103

acceptanceCriteria.description: TypeScript compilation passes with zero errors after implementation
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: bunx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors in packages/data, packages/api, packages/canonical, packages/cli

decisions.title: Use 'replace' as default import mode instead of 'insert'
decisions.description: Changed default import mode from 'insert' to 'replace' (upsert) for better developer experience - most import operations want to update if exists, create if not
decisions.rationale: Upsert is the most common use case and reduces errors from duplicate key violations

decisions.title: Rename import modes to match MoneyWorks REST API
decisions.description: Changed from 'create/update/createOrUpdate' to 'insert/update/replace' to match MoneyWorks REST API terminology exactly
decisions.rationale: Canonical DSL purity - use exact MoneyWorks terms

learnings.title: TSV import requires header row with field names
learnings.description: When importing to MoneyWorks, include field names as the first row of TSV data. MoneyWorks uses this to map columns to fields.
